raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 2903 aims to enhance transparency and accountability in California's efforts to combat homelessness.",
    "The bill mandates that state agencies administering homelessness programs report cost and outcome information annually.",
    "Data from homelessness programs will be publicly accessible by April 1, 2026.",
    "California has allocated over $24 billion towards homelessness initiatives in the last five years.",
    "Despite substantial investments, the number of unhoused individuals in California has continued to rise, exceeding 181,000.",
    "The State Auditor report highlighted a lack of sufficient data to determine the effectiveness of programs aimed at reducing homelessness.",
    "Cal-ICH has not tracked and reported on the State’s funding for homelessness programs since its 2023 assessment.",
    "Cal-ICH currently has no plans to perform a similar assessment in the future.",
    "The absence of an up-to-date assessment makes it challenging to understand ongoing costs and achieved outcomes of homelessness programs.",
    "Cal-ICH has not established a consistent method for gathering information on homelessness programs’ costs and outcomes.",
    "The lack of information prevents the State from making data-driven policy decisions and identifying gaps in services.",
    "Cal-ICH has neither ensured the accuracy of information in the state data system nor used it to evaluate the success of homelessness programs.",
    "The absence of data makes it challenging to evaluate whether homelessness initiatives are successful, need adjustments, or should be discontinued."
  ]
}
```
